name: Voice Control System (Modes, Moves, Retrieval, Rubric)
description: Evolve LinkedIn reference blocks into a best-in-class voice control system: modes, moves library, anti-trope constitution, debranding, and evaluation rubric, wired into existing voice-library-v2 infrastructure.
objective: Enable low-review, high-fidelity generation that moves like V across LinkedIn posts, emails, and memos, with professionalism as the primary adjustable variable.
key_decisions: Voice stored at 3 resolutions: Atoms (phrases), Moves (thinking maneuvers), Monologues (reference blocks)
Do/Don't constitution includes explicit anti-tropes; exclude Not-X-But-Y as a reusable template but allow individual instances
Retrieval must be by rhetorical job + topic, not just semantic similarity
Debrand by role/entity abstraction while preserving motion and cadence
Evaluation via lightweight voice-fit rubric; aim for minimum ongoing review

workers.description: Derive 2-4 voice modes from the LinkedIn corpus (and existing reference blocks) and define how professionalism level modulates tone without changing core voice.
Extract and formalize a Moves Library (thinking maneuvers) from the corpus. Output moves with: name, intent, structure, examples, anti-examples.
Create Do/Don't voice constitution (including anti-tropes). Must be actionable for generation prompts.
Design debranding methodology and create a small set of debranded RB variants that preserve motion/cadence. Include entity abstraction rules and redaction heuristics.
Design a 30-second voice-fit rubric and a minimal review loop. Provide scoring sheet template and thresholds.
Define retrieval spec: how to select RBs/Atoms/Moves by topic + rhetorical job, plus tie-breakers and fallbacks. Output a schema and query strategy.

Inspect existing Vibe Writer wiring (prompts/scripts) and propose the smallest integration plan to consume: modes, moves, constitution, rubric, retrieval spec. NO implementation; only plan + affected files.
